banner: Week 1 · Your Digital Compass — FreeCAD & Measuring
essential_question: How do we turn a real object into exact numbers — and back?
objective: Open FreeCAD and place a point and a line; measure three real objects and record lengths with units and honest precision.

content.heading: A ruler and a computer
bullets: FreeCAD is a compass and straightedge that never slips — we'll build real solids in it later.
A tape measure / calipers turn the world into numbers.
Today: get both working with something simple.

content.heading: Open a Sketch, place geometry
content.note: Open FreeCAD → new document → Sketcher → new sketch on the XY plane. Place a Point. Draw a Line (a segment). Add a length constraint and watch the number update. Save it as 'orientation-first-sketch'. Screenshot for your Portfolio.

content.preview: Next session: how this class runs + your goals.

success_criteria.text: I can open FreeCAD and place a point and a constrained line
I can measure a real object and record length with a unit and sensible precision

questions.prompt: Measure three objects (a book, a table edge, your hand span). Record each length with a unit.
questions.answer: Three lengths, each with a unit (cm or in).
questions.explain: Rubric: three measurements, each with a number AND a unit; realistic values.

questions.prompt: Which of your three measurements was hardest to make precise, and why?
questions.answer: Open — e.g. the hand span, because the ends aren't sharp points.
questions.explain: Full credit: names a real source of imprecision (soft ends, bending tape, reading between marks).

questions.prompt: Reflect: what could you do in FreeCAD that you couldn't do freehand yesterday?
